title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. erectum
intro:
Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. erectum, also known as the Yellow Button or Eastern Daisy Bush, is a perennial plant native to eastern Australia, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and woody stems.

The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Australian Aboriginal culture, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.

The health benefits of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. erectum are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi, making it a potential treatment for skin conditions and infections.

The herbal preparations of Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. erectum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
